skill.name: create-pr
skill.description: Create pull requests following Sentry conventions. Use when opening PRs, writing PR descriptions, or preparing changes for review. Follows Sentry's code review guidelines.
skill.summary: Automate pull request creation with Sentry-compliant descriptions and proper GitHub CLI integration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 36 external command patterns and 3 network references, but all are false positives. The skill uses GitHub CLI (gh) and git commands with hardcoded arguments for legitimate PR creation. Network references are documentation URLs only. No user input injection vectors or data exfiltration risks detected.

content.user_title: Create pull requests with Sentry-compliant descriptions
content.value_statement: Writing effective PR descriptions that follow team conventions takes time and consistency. This skill automates PR creation using GitHub CLI with Sentry's proven review guidelines.
actual_capabilities: Detect uncommitted changes and prompt for commit before PR creation
Analyze git diff and commit history between branch and base
Generate structured PR descriptions following Sentry conventions
Create draft PRs with conventional commit-style titles
Update existing PRs using GitHub API when needed
Reference issues using proper syntax (Fixes, Refs)
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed and authenticated
Cannot create PRs if changes are not committed to a branch
Does not automatically select reviewers or assign PRs
Relies on git history quality for context analysis

use_cases.title: Feature Development PR
use_cases.description: Create a PR for a new feature with clear motivation, alternative approaches considered, and relevant issue references.

use_cases.title: Bug Fix PR
use_cases.description: Quickly create a focused bug fix PR that explains the root cause, the fix, and links to the tracking issue.

use_cases.title: Refactoring PR
use_cases.description: Document code reorganization with before/after context and explain why the refactor enables future work.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pull request for my current branch. Analyze the changes and write a description following Sentry conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Feature PR with context
prompt_templates.prompt: I've implemented feature X. Create a PR that explains what the feature does, why it was needed, and any trade-offs considered.

prompt_templates.title: Bug fix with issue link
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PR for this hotfix. Make sure to include 'Fixes SENTRY-1234' so the issue closes automatically on merge.

prompt_templates.title: Large refactor documentation
prompt_templates.description: I refactored the authentication module across 5 files. Create a PR that clearly explains the scope and assures reviewers there are no behavior changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PR for this refactoring. Emphasize that there are no behavior changes and explain what motivated the reorganization.

output_examples.input: User has commits adding Slack thread notification feature
output_examples.output:
Created draft PR: 'feat(alerts): Add Slack thread replies for notifications'

PR Description:
- Explains threading behavior vs message edits
- Documents timeline preservation benefit
- References SENTRY-1234

output_examples.input: User fixed null handling crash in API endpoint
output_examples.output:
Created draft PR: 'fix(api): Handle null response in user endpoint'

PR Description:
- Describes crash scenario
- Explains null check implementation
- Links to investigation ticket SENTRY-5678

best_practices: Keep PRs focused on a single feature or fix for faster reviews
Explain the 'why' behind changes, not just what the code does
Use draft PRs early when you want feedback on incomplete work
anti_patterns: Bundling unrelated changes into one large PR
Writing vague titles like 'Update code' or 'Fix stuff'
Skipping the PR description entirely and leaving reviewers to guess

faq.question: What do I need before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need GitHub CLI (gh) installed and authenticated with `gh auth login`. All your changes should also be committed before creating a PR.

faq.question: Does this skill work with non-GitHub repositories?
faq.answer: No, this skill is specifically designed for GitHub repositories and uses the GitHub CLI (gh) which only works with GitHub.

faq.question: What happens if I have uncommitted changes?
faq.answer: The skill will detect uncommitted changes and prompt you to run the commit skill first. PRs should only include committed changes.

faq.question: Can this skill update an existing PR?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can update existing PRs using the GitHub API directly, which is more reliable than gh pr edit for certain operations.

faq.question: What PR title format does this use?
faq.answer: Conventional commits format: 'type(scope): description'. Examples: 'feat(auth): Add OAuth support' or 'fix(api): Handle timeout errors'.

faq.question: Does this automatically select reviewers?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses on PR creation and description. Reviewer selection should be done manually based on team processes.
